title: SafeCREW Analytical Protocol #3 – Test protocol for effect-based in-vitro toxicity assessment of disinfection by-products
author: Behnisch, Peter Alexander
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This guideline provides a procedure to evaluate a comprehensive panel of human cell-based biological detection methods (i.e. CALUX® assay) to assess the impact of disinfection by-products (DBPs), related chemicals and chemical mixtures on a range of key types of toxicity pathways (e.g. cytotoxicity, genotoxicity, oxidative stress, endocrine effects, PAH, obesity and competition for TTR-thyroid hormone binding/PFAS-like toxic activities) in all kinds of waters such as a) in contact with pipe materials, b) treated ground- and river water from different drinking water treatment facilities.
steps_raw: This guideline provides a procedure to evaluate a comprehensive panel of human cell-based biological detection methods (i.e. CALUX® assay) to assess the impact of disinfection by-products (DBPs), related chemicals and chemical mixtures on a range of key types of toxicity pathways (e.g. cytotoxicity, genotoxicity, oxidative stress, endocrine effects, PAH, obesity and competition for TTR-thyroid hormone binding/PFAS-like toxic activities) in all kinds of waters such as a) in contact with pipe materials, b) treated ground- and river water from different drinking water treatment facilities.
